title: carduncellus hispanicus subsp. araneosus
intro: Carduncellus hispanicus subsp. araneosus, also known as the Spanish artichoke or artichoke thistle, is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its artichoke-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Carduncellus hispanicus subsp. araneosus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and support kidney function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture for its medicinal purposes, with the dried leaves and flowers often used in herbal remedies.
